claims.summary: A connected telemetry socket must be distinguishable from one that is connected but no longer delivering valid payloads.
claims.disposition: verify BridgeState already records latest telemetry arrival, heartbeat delta, first-valid-payload lag, and freshness evidence in telemetry_port.rs, evidence.rs, and health_trace.rs

claims.summary: Rapid disconnects should use bounded reconnect backoff without losing per-connection liveness identity.
claims.disposition: verify current Backoff state and WebSocket tests cover doubling, reset after valid payload, and first-valid-payload reset on reconnect

claims.summary: Shutdown should remain responsive even when the telemetry stream is delayed or silent.
claims.disposition: route an exact paused-time delayed-ws_rx plus shutdown regression; current source selects shutdown both during connection and reconnect sleep, but the requested interleaving deserves its own bounded test
